title: linum usitatissimum var. ambiguum
intro: Linum usitatissimum var. ambiguum, commonly known as flax, is a plant species within the family Linaceae. It is a type of flowering plant known for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which are rich in alpha-linolenic acid (ALA) and other beneficial compounds. The seeds of Linum usitatissimum var. ambiguum have been traditionally used to support heart health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, among other health benefits. Flaxseed is often consumed in the form of ground seeds, oil, or as a dietary supplement, and can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules.
